message:
runtime_compat: thread the watcher cx through spawn_with_cx in process::cancellable_watcher

The watcher task inside `process` (the spawned loop that polls
`watcher_cx.is_cancel_requested()` and sets the `watcher_cancel` flag
for the child-process supervisor) was still being started with
`super::task::spawn(async move { ... })`. Under asupersync this means
the watcher never has a child-rooted Cx installed in its task context;
it only reads the outer `watcher_cx` it captured, which is fine for
cancellation-check correctness but leaves the task outside the
cancellation chain a spawn_with_cx parent would expect.

Switch to `super::task::spawn_with_cx(&watcher_spawn_cx, move |_child_cx| ...)`,
where `watcher_spawn_cx` is a clone of the already-cloned `watcher_cx`.
The body keeps using the captured `watcher_cx` for the
`is_cancel_requested()` poll; the new `_child_cx` is deliberately
unused at the body level — its purpose is to make the spawn itself
part of the cancellation graph, matching how the other process-side
helpers are spawned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
